    84|
    85|
    86|class WebAuthError(Exception):
    87|    pass
    88|
    89|
    90|class WebCsrfError(Exception):
    91|    pass
    92|
    93|
    94|class WebSessionExpired(WebAuthError):
    95|    pass
    96|
    97|
    98|def _utc_now() -> datetime:
    99|    return datetime.now(timezone.utc)
   100|
   101|
   102|def _db_ts(value: datetime) -> str:
   103|    return value.astimezone(timezone.utc).replace(microsecond=0).isoformat().replace("+00:00", "Z")
   104|
   105|
   106|def _parse_db_ts(value: str) -> datetime:
   107|    normalized = value.replace("Z", "+00:00")
   108|    parsed = datetime.fromisoformat(normalized)
   109|    if parsed.tzinfo is None:
   110|        parsed = parsed.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
   111|    return parsed.astimezone(timezone.utc)
   112|
   113|
   114|def _sign_login_csrf(token: str) -> str:
   115|    signature = hmac.new(jwt_secret().encode("utf-8"), token.encode("utf-8"), hashlib.sha256).hexdigest()
   116|    return f"{token}.{signature}"
   117|
   118|
   119|def _verify_login_csrf_cookie(raw_cookie: str | None) -> str | None:
   120|    if not raw_cookie or "." not in raw_cookie:
   121|        return None
   122|    token, signature = raw_cookie.rsplit(".", 1)
   123|    expected = hmac.new(jwt_secret().encode("utf-8"), token.encode("utf-8"), hashlib.sha256).hexdigest()
   124|    if not hmac.compare_digest(signature, expected):
   125|        return None
   126|    return token
   127|
   128|
   129|def mint_login_csrf_token() -> str:
   130|    return secrets.token_urlsafe(32)
   131|
   132|
   133|def render_login_page() -> LoginPageData:
   134|    return LoginPageData(csrf_token=mint_login_csrf_token())
   135|
   136|
   137|async def login_admin_session(username: str, password: str, form_csrf_token: str | None, cookie_csrf_value: str | None) -> WebSession:
   138|    cookie_token = _verify_login_csrf_cookie(cookie_csrf_value)
   139|    if not form_csrf_token or not cookie_token or not hmac.compare_digest(form_csrf_token, cookie_token):
   140|        raise WebCsrfError("Invalid CSRF token")
   141|    if not validate_admin_credentials(username, password):
   142|        raise WebAuthError("Invalid username or password")
   143|
   144|    now = _utc_now()
   145|    session = WebSession(
   146|        session_id=secrets.token_urlsafe(32),
   147|        admin_jwt=generate_admin_jwt(),
   148|        csrf_secret=secrets.token_urlsafe(32),
   149|        created_at=now,
   150|        last_seen_at=now,
   151|        expires_at=now + timedelta(hours=WEB_ABSOLUTE_TTL_HOURS),
   152|    )
   153|    async with transaction() as db:
   154|        await db.execute("DELETE FROM web_sessions")
   155|        await db.execute(
   156|            """
   157|            INSERT INTO web_sessions(session_id, admin_jwt, csrf_secret, created_at, last_seen_at, expires_at)
   158|            VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
   159|            """,
   160|            (
   161|                session.session_id,
   162|                session.admin_jwt,
   163|                session.csrf_secret,
   164|                _db_ts(session.created_at),
   165|                _db_ts(session.last_seen_at),
   166|                _db_ts(session.expires_at),
   167|            ),
   168|        )
   169|    return session
   170|
   171|
   172|async def _session_from_row(session_id: str) -> WebSession | None:
   173|    db = await get_db()
   174|    cursor = await db.execute("SELECT * FROM web_sessions WHERE session_id = ?", (session_id,))
   175|    row = await cursor.fetchone()
   176|    if row is None:
   177|        return None
   178|    return WebSession(
   179|        session_id=row["session_id"],
   180|        admin_jwt=row["admin_jwt"],
   181|        csrf_secret=row["csrf_secret"],
   182|        created_at=_parse_db_ts(row["created_at"]),
   183|        last_seen_at=_parse_db_ts(row["last_seen_at"]),
   184|        expires_at=_parse_db_ts(row["expires_at"]),
   185|    )
   186|
   187|
   188|async def get_authenticated_session(request: Request) -> WebSession:
   189|    session_id = request.cookies.get(WEB_SESSION_COOKIE_NAME)
   190|    if not session_id:
   191|        raise WebAuthError("Missing session")
   192|    session = await _session_from_row(session_id)
   193|    if session is None:
   194|        raise WebAuthError("Session not found")
   195|    now = _utc_now()
   196|    idle_deadline = session.last_seen_at + timedelta(hours=WEB_IDLE_TTL_HOURS)
   197|    if now > idle_deadline or now > session.expires_at:
   198|        async with transaction() as db:
   199|            await db.execute("DELETE FROM web_sessions WHERE session_id = ?", (session.session_id,))
   200|        raise WebSessionExpired("Session expired")
   201|    async with transaction() as db:
   202|        await db.execute(
   203|            "UPDATE web_sessions SET last_seen_at = ? WHERE session_id = ?",
   204|            (_db_ts(now), session.session_id),
   205|        )
   206|    session.last_seen_at = now
   207|    return session
   208|
   209|
   210|async def logout_admin_session(session_id: str | None) -> None:
   211|    if not session_id:
   212|        return
   213|    async with transaction() as db:
   214|        await db.execute("DELETE FROM web_sessions WHERE session_id = ?", (session_id,))
   215|
   216|
   217|async def get_dashboard_data(_: WebSession) -> DashboardPageData:
   218|    identity = Identity(id="admin", role="admin")
   219|    overview = await control_overview(identity)
   220|    profiles = await control_profiles(active_only=False, limit=500, offset=0, _=identity)
   221|    total_profiles = len(profiles)
   222|    active_profiles = sum(1 for item in profiles if item.is_active)
   223|    return DashboardPageData(
   224|        overview=overview,
   225|        total_profiles=total_profiles,
   226|        active_profiles=active_profiles,
   227|    )
   228|
   229|
   230|async def get_queue_page_data(_: WebSession, queue: str, assignee_id: str | None = None) -> QueuePageData:
   231|    identity = Identity(id="admin", role="admin")
   232|    tasks = await control_exception_tasks(queue=queue, assignee_id=assignee_id, limit=50, offset=0, _=identity)
   233|    return QueuePageData(queue=queue, assignee_id=assignee_id, tasks=tasks)
   234|
   235|
   236|async def get_profiles_page_data(_: WebSession) -> ProfilesPageData:
   237|    identity = Identity(id="admin", role="admin")
   238|    profiles = await control_profiles(active_only=False, limit=500, offset=0, _=identity)
   239|    return ProfilesPageData(profiles=profiles)
   240|
   241|
   242|async def patch_profile_from_form(_: WebSession, profile_id: str, is_active_raw: str | None, routing_metadata_raw: str | None) -> None:
   243|    identity = Identity(id="admin", role="admin")
   244|    routing_metadata: dict[str, Any] | None = None
   245|    if routing_metadata_raw and routing_metadata_raw.strip():
   246|        parsed = json.loads(routing_metadata_raw)
   247|        if not isinstance(parsed, dict):
   248|            raise WebAuthError("routing_metadata must be a JSON object")
   249|        routing_metadata = parsed
   250|    is_active = None
   251|    if is_active_raw is not None:
   252|        is_active = is_active_raw.lower() == "true"
   253|    await patch_control_profile(
   254|        profile_id=profile_id,
   255|        payload=ControlProfilePatchRequest(is_active=is_active, routing_metadata=routing_metadata),
   256|        identity=identity,
   257|    )
   258|
   259|
   260|async def get_task_detail_data(_: WebSession, task_id: str) -> TaskDetailPageData:
   261|    identity = Identity(id="admin", role="admin")
   262|    task = await get_task(task_id, identity)
   263|    runs = await list_task_runs(task_id, limit=50, identity=identity)
   264|    events = await list_task_events(task_id, limit=100, identity=identity)
   265|    messages = await get_task_messages(task_id, limit=50, identity=identity)
   266|    scratchpad = await get_task_scratchpad(task_id, identity=identity)
   267|    return TaskDetailPageData(task=task, runs=runs, events=events, messages=messages, scratchpad=scratchpad)
   268|
   269|
   270|async def reassign_task_from_form(_: WebSession, task_id: str, assignee_id: str, reason: str = "") -> None:
   271|    identity = Identity(id="admin", role="admin")
   272|    await reassign_task(task_id, TaskReassignRequest(assignee_id=assignee_id, reason=reason, metadata={}), identity)

messages.content:
{"success": true, "name": "tdd-workflow", "description": "Use this skill when writing new features, fixing bugs, or refactoring code. Enforces test-driven development with 80%+ coverage including unit, integration, and E2E tests.", "tags": [], "related_skills": [], "content": "---
name: tdd-workflow
description: Use this skill when writing new features, fixing bugs, or refactoring code. Enforces test-driven development with 80%+ coverage including unit, integration, and E2E tests.
origin: ECC
---

# Test-Driven Development Workflow

This skill ensures all code development follows TDD principles with comprehensive test coverage.

## When to Activate

- Writing new features or functionality
- Fixing bugs or issues
- Refactoring existing code
- Adding API endpoints
- Creating new components

## Core Principles

### 1. Tests BEFORE Code
ALWAYS write tests first, then implement code to make tests pass.

### 2. Coverage Requirements
- Minimum 80% coverage (unit + integration + E2E)
- All edge cases covered
- Error scenarios tested
- Boundary conditions verified

### 3. Test Types

#### Unit Tests
- Individual functions and utilities
- Component logic
- Pure functions
- Helpers and utilities

#### Integration Tests
- API endpoints
- Database operations
- Service interactions
- External API calls

#### E2E Tests (Playwright)
- Critical user flows
- Complete workflows
- Browser automation
- UI interactions

### 4. Git Checkpoints
- If the repository is under Git, create a checkpoint commit after each TDD stage
- Do not squash or rewrite these checkpoint commits until the workflow is complete
- Each checkpoint commit message must describe the stage and the exact evidence captured
- Count only commits created on the current active branch for the current task
- Do not treat commits from other branches, earlier unrelated work, or distant branch history as valid checkpoint evidence
- Before treating a checkpoint as satisfied, verify that the commit is reachable from the current `HEAD` on the active branch and belongs to the current task sequence
- The preferred compact workflow is:
  - one commit for failing test added and RED validated
  - one commit for minimal fix applied and GREEN validated
  - one optional commit for refactor complete
- Separate evidence-only commits are not required if the test commit clearly corresponds to RED and the fix commit clearly corresponds to GREEN

## TDD Workflow Steps

### Step 1: Write User Journeys
```
As a [role], I want to [action], so that [benefit]

Example:
As a user, I want to search for markets semantically,
so that I can find relevant markets even without exact keywords.
```

### Step 2: Generate Test Cases
For each user journey, create comprehensive test cases:

```typescript
describe('Semantic Search', () => {
  it('returns relevant markets for query', async () => {
    // Test implementation
  })

  it('handles empty query gracefully', async () => {
    // Test edge case
  })

  it('falls back to substring search when Redis unavailable', async () => {
    // Test fallback behavior
  })

  it('sorts results by similarity score', async () => {
    // Test sorting logic
  })
})
```

### Step 3: Run Tests (They Should Fail)
```bash
npm test
# Tests should fail - we haven't implemented yet
```

This step is mandatory and is the RED gate for all production changes.

Before modifying business logic or other production code, you must verify a valid RED state via one of these paths:
- Runtime RED:
  - The relevant test target compiles successfully
  - The new or changed test is actually executed
  - The result is RED
- Compile-time RED:
  - The new test newly instantiates, references, or exercises the buggy code path
  - The compile failure is itself the intended RED signal
- In either case, the failure is caused by the intended business-logic bug, undefined behavior, or missing implementation
- The failure is not caused only by unrelated syntax errors, broken test setup, missing dependencies, or unrelated regressions

A test that was only written but not compiled and executed does not count as RED.

Do not edit production code until this RED state is confirmed.

If the repository is under Git, create a checkpoint commit immediately after this stage is validated.
Recommended commit message format:
- `test: add reproducer for <feature or bug>`
- This commit may also serve as the RED validation checkpoint if the reproducer was compiled and executed and failed for the intended reason
- Verify that this checkpoint commit is on the current active branch before continuing
- In a freshly initialized repo, verify `.gitignore` exists before running dependency installation or tests, and inspect `git status --short` before committing the RED checkpoint
- Do not use `git add .` blindly for RED/GREEN checkpoints when local artifacts may exist; stage only intended source/test/doc files and `.gitignore`

### Step 4: Implement Code
Write minimal code to make tests pass:

```typescript
// Implementation guided by tests
export async function searchMarkets(query: string) {
  // Implementation here
}
```

If the repository is under Git, stage the minimal fix now but defer the checkpoint commit until GREEN is validated in Step 5.

### Step 5: Run Tests Again
```bash
npm test
# Tests should now pass
```

Rerun the same relevant test target after the fix and confirm the previously failing test is now GREEN.

Only after a valid GREEN result may you proceed to refactor.

If the repository is under Git, create a checkpoint commit immediately after GREEN is validated.
Recommended commit message format:
- `fix: <feature or bug>`
- The fix commit may also serve as the GREEN validation checkpoint if the same relevant test target was rerun and passed
- Verify that this checkpoint commit is on the current active branch before continuing

### Step 6: Refactor
Improve code quality while keeping tests green:
- Remove duplication
- Improve naming
- Optimize performance
- Enhance readability

If the repository is under Git, create a checkpoint commit immediately after refactoring is complete and tests remain green.
Recommended commit message format:
- `refactor: clean up after <feature or bug> implementation`
- Verify that this checkpoint commit is on the current active branch before considering the TDD cycle complete

### Step 7: Verify Coverage
```bash
npm run test:coverage
# Verify 80%+ coverage achieved
```

## Testing Patterns

### Unit Test Pattern (Jest/Vitest)
```typescript
import { render, screen, fireEvent } from '@testing-library/react'
import { Button } from './Button'

describe('Button Component', () => {
  it('renders with correct text', () => {
    render(<Button>Click me</Button>)
    expect(screen.getByText('Click me')).toBeInTheDocument()
  })

  it('calls onClick when clicked', () => {
    const handleClick = jest.fn()
    render(<Button onClick={handleClick}>Click</Button>)

    fireEvent.click(screen.getByRole('button'))

    expect(handleClick).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1)
  })

  it('is disabled when disabled prop is true', () => {
    render(<Button disabled>Click</Button>)
    expect(screen.getByRole('button')).toBeDisabled()
  })
})
```

### API Integration Test Pattern
```typescript
import { NextRequest } from 'next/server'
import { GET } from './route'

describe('GET /api/markets', () => {
  it('returns markets successfully', async () => {
    const request = new NextRequest('http://localhost/api/markets')
    const response = await GET(request)
    const data = await response.json()

    expect(response.status).toBe(200)
    expect(data.success).toBe(true)
    expect(Array.isArray(data.data)).toBe(true)
  })

  it('validates query parameters', async () => {
    const request = new NextRequest('http://localhost/api/markets?limit=invalid')
    const response = await GET(request)

    expect(response.status).toBe(400)
  })

  it('handles database errors gracefully', async () => {
    // Mock database failure
    const request = new NextRequest('http://localhost/api/markets')
    // Test error handling
  })
})
```

### E2E Test Pattern (Playwright)
```typescript
import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test'

test('user can search and filter markets', async ({ page }) => {
  // Navigate to markets page
  await page.goto('/')
  await page.click('a[href="/markets"]')

  // Verify page loaded
  await expect(page.locator('h1')).toContainText('Markets')

  // Search for markets
  await page.fill('input[placeholder="Search markets"]', 'election')

  // Wait for debounce and results
  await page.waitForTimeout(600)

  // Verify search results displayed
  const results = page.locator('[data-testid="market-card"]')
  await expect(results).toHaveCount(5, { timeout: 5000 })

  // Verify results contain search term
  const firstResult = results.first()
  await expect(firstResult).toContainText('election', { ignoreCase: true })

  // Filter by status
  await page.click('button:has-text("Active")')

  // Verify filtered results
  await expect(results).toHaveCount(3)
})

test('user can create a new market', async ({ page }) => {
  // Login first
  await page.goto('/creator-dashboard')

  // Fill market creation form
  await page.fill('input[name="name"]', 'Test Market')
  await page.fill('textarea[name="description"]', 'Test description')
  await page.fill('input[name="endDate"]', '2025-12-31')

  // Submit form
  await page.click('button[type="submit"]')

  // Verify success message
  await expect(page.locator('text=Market created successfully')).toBeVisible()

  // Verify redirect to market page
  await expect(page).toHaveURL(/\/markets\/test-market/)
})
```

## Test File Organization

```
src/
├── components/
│   ├── Button/
│   │   ├── Button.tsx
│   │   ├── Button.test.tsx          # Unit tests
│   │   └── Button.stories.tsx       # Storybook
│   └── MarketCard/
│       ├── MarketCard.tsx
│       └── MarketCard.test.tsx
├── app/
│   └── api/
│       └── markets/
│           ├── route.ts
│           └── route.test.ts         # Integration tests
└── e2e/
    ├── markets.spec.ts               # E2E tests
    ├── trading.spec.ts
    └── auth.spec.ts
```

## Mocking External Services

### Supabase Mock
```typescript
jest.mock('@/lib/supabase', () => ({
  supabase: {
    from: jest.fn(() => ({
      select: jest.fn(() => ({
        eq: jest.fn(() => Promise.resolve({
          data: [{ id: 1, name: 'Test Market' }],
          error: null
        }))
      }))
    }))
  }
}))
```

### Redis Mock
```typescript
jest.mock('@/lib/redis', () => ({
  searchMarketsByVector: jest.fn(() => Promise.resolve([
    { slug: 'test-market', similarity_score: 0.95 }
  ])),
  checkRedisHealth: jest.fn(() => Promise.resolve({ connected: true }))
}))
```

### OpenAI Mock
```typescript
jest.mock('@/lib/openai', () => ({
  generateEmbedding: jest.fn(() => Promise.resolve(
    new Array(1536).fill(0.1) // Mock 1536-dim embedding
  ))
}))
```

## Test Coverage Verification

### Run Coverage Report
```bash
npm run test:coverage
```

### Coverage Thresholds
```json
{
  "jest": {
    "coverageThresholds": {
      "global": {
        "branches": 80,
        "functions": 80,
        "lines": 80,
        "statements": 80
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Common Testing Mistakes to Avoid

### FAIL: WRONG: Testing Implementation Details
```typescript
// Don't test internal state
expect(component.state.count).toBe(5)
```

### PASS: CORRECT: Test User-Visible Behavior
```typescript
// Test what users see
expect(screen.getByText('Count: 5')).toBeInTheDocument()
```

### FAIL: WRONG: Brittle Selectors
```typescript
// Breaks easily
await page.click('.css-class-xyz')
```

### PASS: CORRECT: Semantic Selectors
```typescript
// Resilient to changes
await page.click('button:has-text("Submit")')
await page.click('[data-testid="submit-button"]')
```

### FAIL: WRONG: No Test Isolation
```typescript
// Tests depend on each other
test('creates user', () => { /* ... */ })
test('updates same user', () => { /* depends on previous test */ })
```

### PASS: CORRECT: Independent Tests
```typescript
// Each test sets up its own data
test('creates user', () => {
  const user = createTestUser()
  // Test logic
})

test('updates user', () => {
  const user = createTestUser()
  // Update logic
})
```

## Continuous Testing

### Watch Mode During Development
```bash
npm test -- --watch
# Tests run automatically on file changes
```

### Pre-Commit Hook
```bash
# Runs before every commit
npm test && npm run lint
```

### CI/CD Integration
```yaml
# GitHub Actions
- name: Run Tests
  run: npm test -- --coverage
- name: Upload Coverage
  uses: codecov/codecov-action@v3
```

## Best Practices

1. **Write Tests First** - Always TDD
2. **One Assert Per Test** - Focus on single behavior
3. **Descriptive Test Names** - Explain what's tested
4. **Arrange-Act-Assert** - Clear test structure
5. **Mock External Dependencies** - Isolate unit tests
6. **Test Edge Cases** - Null, undefined, empty, large
7. **Test Error Paths** - Not just happy paths
8. **Keep Tests Fast** - Unit tests < 50ms each
9. **Clean Up After Tests** - No side effects
10. **Review Coverage Reports** - Identify gaps

## Success Metrics

- 80%+ code coverage achieved
- All tests passing (green)
- No skipped or disabled tests
- Fast test execution (< 30s for unit tests)
- E2E tests cover critical user flows
- Tests catch bugs before production

---

**Remember**: Tests are not optional. They are the safety net that enables confident refactoring, rapid development, and production reliability.

","new_string":"async def _load_task_detail_section(
    loader: Callable[[], Awaitable[T]],
    *,
    fallback: T,
    error_message: str,
    timeout_seconds: float = TASK_DETAIL_SECTION_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,
) -> tuple[T, str | None]:
    try:
        value = await asyncio.wait_for(loader(), timeout=timeout_seconds)
    except (asyncio.TimeoutError, HTTPException, RuntimeError):
        return fallback, error_message
    except Exception:
        return fallback, error_message
    return value, None


async def get_task_detail_data(_: WebSession, task_id: str) -> TaskDetailPageData:
    identity = Identity(id="admin", role="admin")
    task = await get_task(task_id, identity)
    runs_result, events_result, messages_result, scratchpad_result = await asyncio.gather(
        _load_task_detail_section(
            lambda: list_task_runs(task_id, limit=TASK_DETAIL_RUNS_LIMIT, identity=identity),
            fallback=[],
            error_message="Runs unavailable",
        ),
        _load_task_detail_section(
            lambda: list_task_events(task_id, limit=TASK_DETAIL_EVENTS_LIMIT, identity=identity),
            fallback=[],
            error_message="Events unavailable",
        ),
        _load_task_detail_section(
            lambda: get_task_messages(task_id, limit=TASK_DETAIL_MESSAGES_LIMIT, identity=identity),
            fallback=[],
            error_message="Messages unavailable",
        ),
        _load_task_detail_section(
            lambda: get_task_scratchpad(task_id, identity=identity),
            fallback={},
            error_message="Scratchpad unavailable",
        ),
    )
    runs, runs_error = runs_result
    events, events_error = events_result
    messages, messages_error = messages_result
    scratchpad, scratchpad_error = scratchpad_result
    return TaskDetailPageData(
        task=task,
        runs=runs,
        events=events,
        messages=messages,
        scratchpad=scratchpad,
        runs_error=runs_error,
        events_error=events_error,
        messages_error=messages_error,
        scratchpad_error=scratchpad_error,
    )
"}

{"mode":"replace","new_string":"@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_web_profiles_page_rejects_invalid_routing_metadata_without_partial_update(client):
    admin_token = await _login_admin_api(client)
    await _create_profile(client, admin_token, "worker", ["communication"])

    login_response = await _web_login(client)
    assert login_response.status_code in {302, 303}

    page = await client.get("/web/profiles")
    assert page.status_code == 200
    csrf_token = _extract_hidden_input(page.text, "csrf_token")

    invalid = await client.post(
        "/web/profiles/worker",
        data={
            "csrf_token": csrf_token,
            "is_active": "false",
            "routing_metadata": '{invalid json}',
        },
        follow_redirects=False,
    )
    assert invalid.status_code == 400
    assert "routing_metadata must be valid JSON" in invalid.text

    refreshed = await client.get("/web/profiles")
    assert refreshed.status_code == 200
    assert "true" in refreshed.text.lower()


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_web_task_detail_degrades_when_messages_section_times_out(client, monkeypatch):
    admin_token = await _login_admin_api(client)
    alpha = await _create_profile(client, admin_token, "alpha", ["communication", "orchestration"])
    beta = await _create_profile(client, admin_token, "beta", ["communication", "orchestration"])
    alpha_token = alpha["access_token"]
    beta_token = beta["access_token"]
    task = await _create_task(client, alpha_token, "Detail degradation", "beta")

    message = await client.post(
        f"/api/threads/{task['thread_id']}/messages",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {beta_token}"},
        json={"body": "hello from beta", "message_type": "message", "metadata": {}},
    )
    assert message.status_code == 201

    import hivex.web_services as web_services

    async def _timeout_messages(task_id: str, limit: int = 50, identity=None):
        raise TimeoutError("boom")

    monkeypatch.setattr(web_services, "get_task_messages", _timeout_messages)

    login_response = await _web_login(client)
    assert login_response.status_code in {302, 303}

    response = await client.get(f"/web/tasks/{task['task_id']}")
    assert response.status_code == 200
    assert "Detail degradation" in response.text
    assert 'data-testid="messages-error"' in response.text
    assert "Messages unavailable" in response.text
    assert 'data-testid="runs-error"' not in response.text


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_web_task_detail_uses_bounded_backend_limits(client, monkeypatch):
    admin_token = await _login_admin_api(client)
    alpha = await _create_profile(client, admin_token, "alpha", ["communication", "orchestration"])
    beta = await _create_profile(client, admin_token, "beta", ["communication", "orchestration"])
    alpha_token = alpha["access_token"]
    task = await _create_task(client, alpha_token, "Bounded detail", "beta")

    captured: dict[str, int] = {}
    import hivex.web_services as web_services

    original_runs = web_services.list_task_runs
    original_events = web_services.list_task_events
    original_messages = web_services.get_task_messages

    async def _capture_runs(task_id: str, limit: int = 0, identity=None):
        captured["runs"] = limit
        return await original_runs(task_id, limit=limit, identity=identity)

    async def _capture_events(task_id: str, limit: int = 0, identity=None):
        captured["events"] = limit
        return await original_events(task_id, limit=limit, identity=identity)

    async def _capture_messages(task_id: str, limit: int = 0, identity=None):
        captured["messages"] = limit
        return await original_messages(task_id, limit=limit, identity=identity)

    monkeypatch.setattr(web_services, "list_task_runs", _capture_runs)
    monkeypatch.setattr(web_services, "list_task_events", _capture_events)
    monkeypatch.setattr(web_services, "get_task_messages", _capture_messages)

    login_response = await _web_login(client)
    assert login_response.status_code in {302, 303}

    response = await client.get(f"/web/tasks/{task['task_id']}")
    assert response.status_code == 200
    assert captured == {"runs": 50, "events": 100, "messages": 50}

{"mode":"replace","new_string":"@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_web_task_detail_uses_bounded_backend_limits(client, monkeypatch):
    admin_token = await _login_admin_api(client)
    alpha = await _create_profile(client, admin_token, "alpha", ["communication", "orchestration"])
    await _create_profile(client, admin_token, "beta", ["communication", "orchestration"])
    alpha_token = alpha["access_token"]
    task = await _create_task(client, alpha_token, "Bounded detail", "beta")

    captured: dict[str, int] = {}
    import hivex.web_services as web_services

    original_runs = web_services.list_task_runs
    original_events = web_services.list_task_events
    original_messages = web_services.get_task_messages

    async def _capture_runs(task_id: str, limit: int = 0, identity=None):
        captured["runs"] = limit
        return await original_runs(task_id, limit=limit, identity=identity)

    async def _capture_events(task_id: str, limit: int = 0, identity=None):
        captured["events"] = limit
        return await original_events(task_id, limit=limit, identity=identity)

    async def _capture_messages(task_id: str, limit: int = 0, identity=None):
        captured["messages"] = limit
        return await original_messages(task_id, limit=limit, identity=identity)

    monkeypatch.setattr(web_services, "list_task_runs", _capture_runs)
    monkeypatch.setattr(web_services, "list_task_events", _capture_events)
    monkeypatch.setattr(web_services, "get_task_messages", _capture_messages)

    login_response = await _web_login(client)
    assert login_response.status_code in {302, 303}

    response = await client.get(f"/web/tasks/{task['task_id']}")
    assert response.status_code == 200
    assert captured == {"runs": 50, "events": 100, "messages": 50}

function.name: patch
function.arguments:
{"mode":"replace","new_string":"### 5. Task detail page
sections:
- task header/meta
- primary actions (reassign / unblock / retry / archive)
- runs
- events
- messages
- scratchpad

### Task detail bounded read contract (frozen)
- `runs` 复用 `GET /api/tasks/{task_id}/runs`，默认 `limit=50`，按既有 contract 保持 `run_id DESC`（newest-first）。
- `events` 复用 `GET /api/tasks/{task_id}/events`，默认 `limit=100`，按既有 contract 保持 `event_id ASC`（oldest-first）。
- `messages` 复用 `GET /api/tasks/{task_id}/messages`，默认 `limit=50`，按既有 contract 保持 thread message `created_at ASC`。
- `scratchpad` 复用 `GET /api/tasks/{task_id}/scratchpad`，仅展示当前快照，不做历史展开。
- v1 首屏不做无限展开；若后续需要查看更多，必须通过显式分页 / view-more 扩展，不能在当前实现中静默突破默认上限。

### Task detail section isolation contract (frozen)
- `task` 主体读取失败时整页失败；因为没有 task header 就无法判定 detail page 的主体对象。
- `runs / events / messages / scratchpad` 采用 **section-level isolation**：各 section 独立读取、独立 timeout、独立 degraded/error 呈现。
- 默认 section timeout budget 冻结为 **2s**；单个 section timeout 或 helper error 时，该 section 渲染 inline degraded/error，而不是让整页失败。
- section failure 不得冒充成功：失败 section 必须显示明确 unavailable/error 文案，其余成功 section 继续正常渲染。
","old_string":"### 5. Task detail page
sections:
- task header/meta
- primary actions (reassign / unblock / retry / archive)
- runs
- events
- messages
- scratchpad
","path":"/Users/woosley/code/hiveX/docs/design/human-web-operator-console.md"}

{"mode":"replace","new_string":"### Server-side / pytest
- login success/failure
- login form missing/invalid bootstrap CSRF returns `403`
- re-login rotates `session_id` + `csrf_secret` and invalidates the previously issued authenticated session
- unauthenticated redirect to `/web/login`
- session expiry (idle and absolute lifetime) redirects to `/web/login`
- logout deletes `web_sessions` row and invalidates follow-up access
- dashboard route renders summary from existing control overview semantics
- queue route renders tasks from existing control queue semantics, including frozen `status` column
- profile patch success/failure
- profile patch invalid `routing_metadata` JSON returns explicit error and does not partially update profile state
- task action form success/conflict/failure
- task detail rendering only uses bounded task-scoped authorized surfaces (`get_task`, runs/events/messages/scratchpad) with frozen default limits
- task detail section timeout/error degrades only the affected section and keeps the rest of the page readable
- `archive` / `retry` / `unblock` / `reassign` failure branches assert no false-success flash and no partial state change
- CSRF token missing/invalid returns `403` before mutation logic runs
- every POST route has authz/session regression coverage
- cookie flags / TTL config are asserted in web response tests
","old_string":"### Server-side / pytest
- login success/failure
- login form missing/invalid bootstrap CSRF returns `403`
- re-login rotates `session_id` + `csrf_secret` and invalidates the previously issued authenticated session
- unauthenticated redirect to `/web/login`
- session expiry (idle and absolute lifetime) redirects to `/web/login`
- logout deletes `web_sessions` row and invalidates follow-up access
- dashboard route renders summary from existing control overview semantics
- queue route renders tasks from existing control queue semantics
- profile patch success/failure
- profile patch invalid `routing_metadata` JSON returns explicit error and does not partially update profile state
- task action form success/conflict/failure
- `archive` / `retry` / `unblock` / `reassign` failure branches assert no false-success flash and no partial state change
- task detail rendering only uses task-scoped authorized surfaces (`get_task`, runs/events/messages/scratchpad)
- CSRF token missing/invalid returns `403` before mutation logic runs
- every POST route has authz/session regression coverage
- cookie flags / TTL config are asserted in web response tests
","path":"/Users/woosley/code/hiveX/docs/design/human-web-operator-console.md"}
